content: Just as language learners use written Reflexion (reflective journaling, per high English language curricula) to correct persistent grammatical and reasoning errors by re-examining their past output against feedback, large language models can integrate curriculum-style structured reflective prompts into their self-reflection mechanisms to improve long-horizon reasoning task performance. Structured Reflexion assignments designed for human learners can be repurposed to template LLM self-reflection that is more consistent and targeted than ad-hoc LLM reflection frameworks.
